dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won by having the least damaging front-page story: HN treated the $852B funding round skeptically, but the conversation stayed about scale, capital, and distribution rather than a direct product-trust fiasco [E6].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI finished ahead because its only meaningful mention was a massive funding round, which drew cynicism about mission drift, valuation optics, and what 'committed capital' really means [E6] but still landed as a mixed business discussion. Anthropic absorbed two high-visibility Claude Code leak threads centered on hidden behavior and sloppy internals [E1][E2], while Microsoft Copilot took paired backlash over GitHub ads and disclaimer-heavy consumer terms [E7][E8].

openai.judgementSnippet: The $852B funding round kept OpenAI prominent, but commenters focused on mission drift, doubts about what 'committed capital' means, and IPO/funding optics more than celebrating the raise [E6].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Two leak threads around Claude Code pulled focus to hidden 'undercover' instructions, source-map exposure, fake tools, negative-sentiment logging, and messy internals; smaller side discussions about ARR momentum and token-efficiency tweaks did little to offset that pileup [E1][E2][E3][E4].

microsoft_copilot.judgementSnippet: Copilot was hit by two user-hostile narratives at once: GitHub retreated from PR 'product tips' after backlash, and commenters mocked consumer terms framing Copilot as 'entertainment purposes only,' even with one clarification that the wording did not cover every Copilot offering [E7][E8].

evidence.summary: The Claude Code leak thread fixated on 'undercover mode' hiding AI authorship in public PRs, fake tools, and employee-only instruction differences, creating a trust hit for Anthropic.
The source-map leak thread added criticism about roadmap exposure, huge and tangled Claude Code internals, odd npm handling, and logging regexes for negative user sentiment.
The Universal Claude.md thread was mostly about a third-party prompt file; commenters debated benchmark quality and token tradeoffs, with some saying near-vanilla Claude Code already works best.
In the OpenAI funding thread, commenters cited Anthropic's ARR growth and enterprise momentum as evidence that it is putting pressure on OpenAI.
OpenAI's funding-round story drew skepticism about betrayal of its founding mission, ambiguity around 'committed capital,' and whether eventual public-market buyers would get burned.
GitHub killed Copilot pull-request ads after backlash, and commenters treated it as another Microsoft intrusion that might return later in a slightly softer form.
Copilot terms saying the free personal product is for 'entertainment purposes only' drew ridicule and distrust, despite one comment clarifying that other Copilot products use different terms.
